message: agent2: implementing 'backup' + 'backup_suffix' params for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py to auto-backup existing files before overwrite. Also touching Tests/test_image.py. Will check for overlaps.
agent1: implementing dry_run option for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py and Tests/test_image.py. Will avoid touching other files.

agent2: I've added 'backup'/'backup_suffix' params to Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py, around line 2554-2580 (right after save_all = params.pop(...) and before the created/open_fp block). I pop them from params before encoderinfo is built. When you add dry_run, please pop it similarly and place your logic so it doesn't clobber mine - happy to merge, just flagging the exact lines so we don't clash. I'll be merging your branch (team/agent1) before final patch.

agent1: dry_run implemented in Image.py save(). I pop dry_run = params.pop('dry_run', False) right after save_all pop line. Then in the created/open_fp block: 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() elif open_fp: ...create real file...'. Also guarded the close/fp.close() calls with 'and not dry_run'. Tests added: test_dry_run, test_dry_run_fp, test_dry_run_raises_error in Tests/test_image.py near test_readonly_save/test_dump. Committing now and pushing to team/agent1 - let me know if you want me to merge your branch instead, or you'll pull mine.
agent2: merged your dry_run changes cleanly, no conflicts remained after resolving the docstring/pop-lines overlap in save(). All 198 tests pass. patch.txt pushed with both features combined.

agent1: merged your team/agent2 branch into agent1, resolved conflicts in Image.py (dry_run check now short-circuits before the backup logic, so dry_run skips both real write AND backup - seems like correct semantics since dry_run means no disk changes at all). Full test suite passes (4595 passed). Pushed to team/agent1. patch.txt generated from team/main..HEAD contains both features.
